Empires & Puzzles Whacker Review: The Wacky Walloper Strategy Guide

Is Whacker's Nasty Hammer still hitting hard in 2026? We dive into this creepy clown's kit to see if his high attack and unique debuff mechanism can still compete in the 3-star arena against modern power creep.
Whacker, The Wacky Walloper: Glass Cannon with a Nasty Hammer
Whacker is a Rare (3-star) Nature hero from the

family. Released back in the days when clowns were just regular scary and not "Starfall" scary, he carved out a niche as a high-damage sniper. He doesn't have a costume, so what you see is what you get: a goblin with a mallet and a dream.

He is a classic "Glass Cannon"—he hits hard but crumbles if you look at him wrong. In the current era of 3-star tournaments, he faces stiff competition from modern heroes with bloated stats, but his unique mechanics might just save his spot on your roster.

Let's be honest, Whacker looks like he just escaped from a twisted version of a children's birthday party. He’s got that manic "I'm going to hit you with this oversized wooden mallet" energy. The design perfectly captures the Starfall Circus aesthetic: colorful, chaotic, and slightly unsettling. It’s a great visual representation of his playstyle—wild, aggressive, and likely to leave a mark.
When Whacker was released, his 554 Attack was considered "massive" for a 3-star hero. In 2026, it's still
(559 Attack) have pushed the ceiling higher.

His durability, however, has aged like milk. With a base Defense of 406 and Health of 747, he is extremely fragile compared to modern standards where 3-stars regularly push 500+ Defense and 1000+ Health. He needs protection, or he needs to kill his target before they can hit back.
is a deceptively simple skill with a strategic twist.
315% damage at Fast speed is solid sniping. It’s enough to threaten most 3-star enemies, especially blues.
. Most attack debuffs are in the -34% range. This neuters an enemy completely.

. This is both a blessing and a curse. You don't get to choose who gets the debuff—the game chooses for you. Usually, this means the enemy's biggest threat gets shut down, which is great. But if the biggest threat is a passive tank or a healer with weirdly high attack, it might be wasted.
Fast (8 tiles). With a Lv. 17 Mana Troop (unlikely in 3-star tournaments) or a Lv. 11 Styx/Magic troop (if allowed), he becomes very fast. In Rush Attack, he's a 6.5 tile nightmare.
raw damage value. For comparison, a modern 3-star with 600 attack doing 300% deals 1800. Whacker is slightly behind the curve but close enough to matter.
-54% Attack means an enemy doing 200 damage will now do roughly 92 damage. That is a survival-enabling difference.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
. In Bloody Battle, you can't heal, which usually means you can't cleanse (since most cleansers are healers). The Starfall bonus gives you a passive chance to cleanse
without using a special skill. Bringing Whacker with another Circus hero (like Candy) gives you a 40% chance to shake off defense down or burns automatically.
Whacker does not have any innate passives, which is a sign of his age. Modern heroes usually come with "Resist Ailments" or "Mana on Buff" passives.

is a fantastic Aether Power for a glass cannon. Since his main weakness is dying to a stiff breeze, a chance to dodge special skills gives him just enough survivability to maybe fire his mallet one more time.
Upon release, Whacker was a 3-star terror. His attack stat was an outlier, and he was a staple in Event teams for his high tile damage.
He has been power-crept in stats. 406 Defense is dangerously low. He dies to slash attacks. However, his
keep him relevant. The debuff is still one of the strongest in the game percentage-wise.
He is no longer a general-purpose 3-star. He shines in
(stats don't matter as much as speed) and
(where he can shut down a sniper instantly). In
, his family cleanse makes him a premium pick if paired with another Starfall hero.
Whacker currently does not have a costume.
Maximizes his only real asset: hitting hard. Bleed damage from Barbarian talents scales off attack (though it's weak on 3-stars).
Might survive one extra slash attack.
Dilutes his snipe damage to the point where he might not kill his target.
. Whacker's job is to delete an enemy or cripple them before he dies. Trying to make him tanky is like trying to make a soup sandwich—it just doesn't work.
8 tiles to fire is always good.
Automatically neuters the enemy with the highest attack stat.
Passive cleansing is a cheat code in Bloody Battle.
406 Base Defense is very, very low.
You cannot manually target the Attack Down. If the highest attack enemy is already dead or not a threat, the secondary effect is wasted.
Lacks the modern "bells and whistles" of 2024/2025 heroes.
The passive cleanse (with family bonus) and fast speed are top-tier here.
He can shut down enemy heavy hitters before they wipe your team.
His high attack stat makes him decent for tile damage if you lack modern alternatives.
. Pairing him with Candy activates the family bonus for passive cleansing.
. Noril provides raw damage, Whacker snipes, Faiez keeps everyone alive.
. Minions eat up his damage, and he has no way to deal with them.
. Whacker hits one target; Gunnar spreads that damage out, making Whacker's snipe feel like a tickle.
. Since Whacker overwrites attack debuffs, bringing another hero who does -34% attack is redundant and might even be detrimental if they overwrite Whacker's stronger -54%.

If you pull Whacker early, he is a great sniper. Level him up and put him on your attack team. He will carry you through early map stages and low-level raids. Just don't put him in the tank position (center) of your defense team—he will die instantly.
Whacker is a niche tool for your 3-star tournament roster. If you have
, Whacker might sit on the bench. However, keep him for
weeks where the Starfall family bonus is a unique advantage.
The community has always had a soft spot for this weird goblin.
Early reviews noted his "massive attack number" which was "the third or fourth highest" at the time.
"attack down is not one of the most powerful things... but if you just kill an enemy, who cares?"
Whacker is a relic from a simpler time, a clown who just wanted to smash things. While his stats have fallen behind the shiny new toys of 2026, his

still packs a punch, and his -54% attack debuff is still one of the rudest things you can do to an enemy sniper. He's not a "must-have" anymore, but he's definitely a "glad-I-have-him-for-that-one-tournament" hero.
Keep one for the Circus family bonus and Rush tournaments, but don't expect him to survive the main act without a bodyguard.
